sections.paragraph: const. שְׁמֵי pl. m. heaven (from the unused sing. שָׁמַי, Arab. سَمَاءُ, Eth. ሰማይ፡, from the root שָׁמָה) i.e. firmament (רָקִיעַ which see) which seems to be spread out like a vault over the globe, as supported on foundations and columns ( 2Sa 22:8 Job 26:11), whence the rain is let down as through doors or flood-gates ( Psa 78:23 compare Gen 28:17, and אֲרֻבֹּות) and above which the abode of God and the angels was supposed to be, Psa 2:4 Gen 28:17 Deu 33:26. With ה local, הַשָּׁמַיְמָה towards heaven, Gen 15:5 28:12 in which sense it is also put in acc. שָׁמַיִם, הַשָּׁמַיִם 1Sa 5:12 Psa 139:8 and עַל הַשָּׁמַיִם Exo 9:23 תַּחַת הַשָּׁמַיִם on earth, Ecc 1:13 2:3 3:1 compare (תַּחַת כָּל־הַשָּׁמַיִם in the whole earth) Job 28:24 37:3 41:3 שָׁמַיִם וּשְׁמֵי שָׁמַיִם heaven and the heaven of heavens, i.e. all the spaces of heaven, however vast and infinite, Deu 10:14 1Ki 8:27 הַשָּׁמַיִם וְהָאָרֶץ heaven and earth, i.e. mundus universus, Gen 1:1 2:1 14:19 , 22 Gen 14:22. In the later books of the Old Test. Jehovah is often called אֱלֹהֵי הַשָּׁמַיִם the God of heaven (see Chald.) 2Ch 36:23 Ezr 1:2 Neh 1:4, 2:4 , 20 Psa 136:26 Jon 1:9 compare יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵי הַשָּׁמַיִם Gen 24:7.
